w:r.w:t: This is the core of the S.A.G.A. Engine. This is where we kill the "Chatbot" and birth the "Game."
. LangGraph is designed specifically for building cyclical state machines. We will wire it so that every single click on the VTT triggers a strict sequential pipeline:
Update State -> Check Roadmap -> Math -> Director Check -> AI Narration -> Update VTT
Here is the exact, developer-ready blueprint for the
🏭 S.A.G.A. BLUEPRINT 09-B: The Director State Machine

A cyclical LangGraph pipeline running inside the Game Master App. It is strictly event-driven. It intercept's the VTT's mechanical API calls (e.g., clicking a map tile, clicking a potion), calculates the math using the other microservices, checks the Campaign Roadmap for triggers, and explicitly commands the AI what to narrate.
This is the State object that passes from node to node in the LangGraph pipeline. Notice how it tracks the "Director Override."
# If populated, the AI MUST narrate this event
3. Internal Logic: The LangGraph Nodes (How to Code It)
We will build exactly 4 nodes. They execute in a strict circle.
This node runs the instant the player clicks the VTT. It fetches the exact truth of the world so the AI never hallucinates.
# 1. Gateway calls to Campaign Manager (Mod 8) and Character Engine (Mod 3)
This node bypasses the AI completely. It looks at what the player clicked, sends it to the math microservices, and records the result.
This is the magic. This node checks the Campaign Roadmap. If the player moved to a specific coordinate, the Director pauses normal play, forces an encounter, and writes a script for the AI.
# If the player is just healing or chatting, do nothing.
# Ask the Campaign Manager (Mod 8): Is there a quest trigger here?
This is the location of the 'Bandit Ambush' quest trigger.
Narrate 3 Wolf Cultists dropping from the trees, weapons drawn.
Demand they hand over their Aetherium coins.]
# Tell the React VTT to lock UI and drop enemy tokens on the map
The AI finally gets to speak. Notice how we staple the Context, the Math, and the Director's instructions together into one unbreakable prompt.
You are the gritty, tactical Game Master of the SAGA Engine.
# 2. Check who is driving the story right now
\nWrite 2 sentences of narration detailing the Mechanical Truth."
This wires the 4 nodes together into a cyclical factory belt.
# Define the exact execution order (The Pipeline)
5. The VTT React Implementation (The Frontend Fix)
To make this work, you must change how your MapCanvas.tsx operates. You do not type to move. You click.
// 1. Instantly move the player token visually on the frontend
// 2. Send the silent API payload to the SAGA Director
// Parse the string and draw the 3 Wolf Cultists on the PixiJS grid
// 4. Display the Narration in the chat log
The AI never forgets you are bleeding out in a Tundra because Node 1 injects that fact into the prompt
every single time you click your mouse

If you try to wander off the map or walk into a quest zone, Node 3 (The Director) intercepts your click. It literally overrides your action, tells the VTT to lock your UI, spawns the enemies, and forces the AI to yell at you.

You play the game by clicking grid squares, clicking inventory items, and clicking skill buttons. The AI just serves as a dynamic, real-time audio/text engine describing the math that the Python factory line generated.
This architecture turns a reactive chatbot into a
You now have the exact code architecture for the Director Engine. Is this the definitive operational loop you want governing the VTT?
